quote: Not everything that counts can be counted, and not everything that can be counted counts.
through_line: The cost of AI is invisible until you look at the data — in electricity, in organic traffic, in the assumption that machines that ‘think’ are neutral tools. Today’s brief asks what it means to see clearly: in Q3 traffic benchmarks, in the energy footprint of a data center, and in the layers of consciousness we carry into every moment of attention.

articles.title: What we know about energy use at US data centers amid the AI boom
articles.pitch: For every AI query answered, there’s a data center running somewhere drawing significant power. Pew Research synthesizes the latest research on AI’s energy footprint — a scale that most conversations about AI’s promise quietly skip past.

articles.title: Q3 2025 Destination Website Performance: Organic Softens, LLM Traffic Grows, Discovery Diversifies
articles.pitch: Q3 2025 confirmed a pattern Q1 only suggested: LLM-driven traffic is growing while organic search softens across destination websites. Tempest’s benchmark data is the clearest read available on where DMO web traffic is actually heading — and why.

articles.title: AI Overviews on Google: How DMOs Can Adapt and Thrive in the New SEO Era
articles.pitch: Google’s AI Overviews are answering travel questions before users ever click a link. Madden Media walks through what that means for DMO content strategies, how to optimize for the new answer-first landscape, and what it actually takes to stay visible in the AI search layer.

articles.title: Screen-Free Computational Thinking in the Elementary Grades
articles.pitch: Computational thinking doesn’t require a computer. This piece gathers physical, unplugged approaches to teaching decomposition, pattern recognition, and algorithmic thinking in elementary classrooms — and makes a quiet case that the thinking matters more than the tool.

articles.title: The Mythology Of Conscious AI
articles.pitch: Neuroscientist Anil Seth argues that most popular discourse about AI consciousness is built on mythologies — misunderstandings about what experience actually is and what it would take to have it. A philosophically rigorous correction that leaves the question genuinely open in the right way.

articles.title: The Four Layers of Consciousness
articles.pitch: Thich Nhat Hanh maps the four layers of consciousness in Buddhist teaching — from the store consciousness where all experience is recorded to the mind consciousness where deliberate thought happens. A framework that changes how you understand your own reactivity and stillness.

articles.title: How ‘nothing’ has inspired art and science for millennia
articles.pitch: Nothing — the void, the vacuum, the empty set — has been one of the most generative concepts in human thought. This Aeon essay traces zero from the terror it provoked in ancient minds to the way modern physics makes nothing the most interesting substance there is.
